nv.description: Melanocytic Nevi (moles) are common benign skin growths formed by clusters of pigment-producing cells.
nv.treatment: Treatment usually not required unless cosmetic removal is desired.
nv.treatment_duration: If removed surgically, healing usually takes 1–3 weeks.
nv.recovery_time: Recovery after removal is typically within a few weeks.
nv.lesion_behavior: Usually permanent but stable and harmless over time.
nv.precaution: Avoid excessive sun exposure and monitor for shape or color changes.
nv.consult: Consult dermatologist if mole changes rapidly or bleeds.
nv.spread: Does not spread to other body parts.
nv.cancer_risk: Very low risk unless abnormal changes occur.
nv.prevention: Use sunscreen regularly and avoid excessive UV exposure.

mel.description: Melanoma is a serious form of skin cancer developing from melanocytes.
mel.severity: serious and potentially life-threatening if untreated
mel.treatment: Requires surgical removal and possibly immunotherapy or radiation.
mel.treatment_duration: Treatment duration depends on cancer stage and therapy response.
mel.recovery_time: Recovery varies from weeks to months depending on treatment stage.
mel.lesion_behavior: Can grow and spread if untreated.
mel.precaution: Avoid sun exposure and monitor suspicious lesions.
mel.spread: Can spread to internal organs if untreated.
mel.cancer_risk: This is a malignant skin cancer.
mel.prevention: Use SPF 30+ sunscreen and avoid tanning beds.

bkl.description: Benign Keratosis includes harmless skin growths such as seborrheic keratosis.
bkl.treatment: Usually no treatment required unless irritation occurs.
bkl.recovery_time: Recovery is quick after removal if treated.
bkl.consult: Consult dermatologist if lesion changes appearance.

bcc.description: Basal Cell Carcinoma is a slow-growing skin cancer that rarely spreads.
bcc.treatment: Treatment includes surgery, cryotherapy, or topical medications.
bcc.treatment_duration: Treatment usually completed within a few weeks.
bcc.recovery_time: Healing typically occurs within 2–6 weeks after treatment.
bcc.precaution: Avoid sun exposure and monitor lesion growth.
bcc.spread: Rarely spreads but may damage nearby tissues.

akiec.description: Actinic Keratosis is a precancerous skin condition caused by sun exposure.
akiec.treatment: Treatment includes topical creams or cryotherapy.
akiec.treatment_duration: Treatment duration ranges from days to weeks depending on therapy.
akiec.recovery_time: Recovery typically occurs within a few weeks.
akiec.lesion_behavior: May persist or progress if untreated.
akiec.spread: Does not spread internally but may progress into cancer.

vasc.description: Vascular lesions are harmless abnormalities of blood vessels.
vasc.treatment: Treatment usually not required unless cosmetic removal needed.
vasc.treatment_duration: Laser removal healing takes about 1 to 2 weeks.

df.description: Dermatofibroma is a benign skin growth caused by minor skin injury.
df.recovery_time: Recovery after removal takes a few weeks.
df.consult: Consult dermatologist if painful or enlarging.
